title: Meet the Robots: Our Helpful New Friends
passage:
Meet the Robots: Our Helpful New Friends

Robots are everywhere today. Many people have robots in their homes. A very popular home robot is the vacuum cleaner robot. This small, round robot cleans the floor. It moves around the room by itself. It can go under tables and chairs. When it has low battery, it goes back to its charger. It helps people save time because they do not have to clean every day.

There are also robots in schools. Some schools use small robots to teach children. Children learn how to build and program these robots. This helps them learn science and computer skills. Some robots can also speak. They help students learn new languages like English or Spanish. Students can talk to the robot, and the robot answers their questions in a friendly voice.

In some hospitals, robots help doctors and nurses. These robots are often tall. They carry heavy medicine and food to patients' rooms. This is good because doctors and nurses are very busy. The robots do not get tired. They can work all day and night without sleeping.

We also have robots in big factories. These robots are very strong. They can build cars and pack big boxes. They do the same job many times without making mistakes. This helps companies make things fast and cheap.

In the future, we will see many more robots. Some scientists are making robots that look like humans. These are called humanoid robots. They have arms, legs, and a face. They can walk, run, and talk. Some people think robots will be our best friends in the future. Other people think robots will do all the difficult work for us. Robots make our lives easier and more interesting.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 1 says: 'When it has low battery, it goes back to its charger.'

vocabulary.meaning: a machine that cleans floors by sucking up dust and dirt
a piece of equipment used to put electricity into a battery
to give a computer or robot a set of instructions to do something
having a shape or appearance like a human
a large building where machines are used to make things

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (Tactics)

1. Định vị từ khóa (Scanning): Trình độ A1-A2 có các câu hỏi sử dụng từ ngữ giống hệt trong bài. Hãy tìm các từ khóa nổi bật như 'charger', 'hospital', 'humanoid', 'factory' để tìm đoạn văn chứa câu trả lời nhanh nhất.

2. Đọc kỹ yêu cầu số lượng từ: Đối với dạng bài điền từ (Sentence Completion), hãy chú ý giới hạn từ: NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS (Không quá 2 từ). Ví dụ, câu 9 đáp án là 'low battery' (2 từ) là hợp lệ.

3. Phân biệt False và Not Given: Nếu thông tin trong câu hỏi ngược lại hoàn toàn với bài đọc, chọn FALSE (ví dụ: robot giống người chứ không phải giống động vật). Nếu bài đọc hoàn toàn không nói đến thông tin đó, chọn NOT GIVEN (ví dụ: việc học nấu ăn bằng robot).
